notes: Split proposal application semantics are anchored by the supervisor apply path and regression tests that mention SG-SPEC-0004 and exercise canonical parent/child materialization.
Retrospective refactor pass semantics are anchored by supervisor graph-health/refactor-queue runtime code and regression tests that mention SG-SPEC-0005.
Tracked proposal lane semantics are anchored by supervisor proposal-lane synchronization runtime code and overlay regression tests that mention SG-SPEC-0006.

Intent mediation and discovery boundary semantics are semantic-surface backed by the runtime evidence registry and a live regression anchor. This trace contract intentionally does not claim executor-code embodiment.

Explicit project-memory consultation semantics are anchored by a supervisor trace anchor and regression tests for the conversation-memory derived surfaces. Policy and viewer contract files remain evidence artifacts, not trace-collected code refs.

OperatorRequest single-run steering semantics are anchored by a supervisor trace anchor and regression tests for packet normalization, targeted refinement, split proposal, and proposal linkage flows. Policy, vocabulary, and manual files remain evidence artifacts, not trace-collected code refs.

Ubiquitous vocabulary meta-ontology primitives are semantic-surface backed by the runtime evidence registry and a live regression anchor. This trace contract intentionally does not claim executor-code embodiment or a completed runtime universal meta-ontology.

Bootstrap relation vocabulary semantics are semantic-surface backed by the runtime evidence registry and a live regression anchor. This trace contract intentionally does not claim executor-code embodiment or a dedicated runtime relation engine.

OperatorRequest context-pack and ambient vocabulary semantics are semantic-surface backed by the runtime evidence registry and a live regression anchor. This trace contract intentionally does not claim executor-code embodiment or a dedicated runtime context-pack resolver.
Retrospective application gate semantics are anchored by supervisor proposal-lane approval gate runtime code and regression tests that mention SG-SPEC-0013.

Spec edge semantics and declared-versus-derived blocking policy are semantic-surface backed by the runtime evidence registry and a live regression anchor. This trace contract intentionally does not claim a dedicated runtime edge-policy engine.

Delegation and atomic decomposition policy is semantic-surface backed by the runtime evidence registry and a live regression anchor, with runtime projection through existing atomicity and split_required checks.

Linked readiness and evidence policy is semantic-surface backed by the runtime evidence registry and a live regression anchor. This trace contract intentionally does not claim a dedicated readiness-transition runtime engine.

Runtime observability entity boundary is semantic-surface backed by the runtime evidence registry and a live regression anchor. This trace contract intentionally does not claim telemetry collection, storage, or instrumentation runtime code.

Adoption-layer entity boundary is semantic-surface backed by the runtime evidence registry and a live regression anchor. This trace contract intentionally does not claim UX taxonomy, analytics, campaign operations, or runtime implementation code.
Canonical revision and supersession lineage semantics are embodied in supervisor lineage reconciliation and regression tests.

Executable-layer traceability boundary is semantic-surface backed by the runtime evidence registry and a live regression anchor. This trace contract intentionally does not claim repository layout, CI workflow, build tooling, deployment, or implementation scanner runtime code.

Derived metric family boundary is semantic-surface backed by the runtime evidence registry and a live regression anchor. This trace contract intentionally does not claim formula execution, threshold policy, dashboards, storage, or scheduler runtime code.

Node maturity semantics are semantic-surface backed by the runtime evidence registry and a live regression anchor. This trace contract intentionally does not claim scoring formulas, threshold gates, dashboard behavior, or scheduler runtime code.
Edge-kind validation semantics are anchored by supervisor dependency, cycle, and changed-spec validation helpers plus regression tests that exercise edge-overlap and readiness behavior.

Canonical provenance envelope semantics are semantic-surface backed by the runtime evidence registry and a live regression anchor. This trace contract intentionally does not claim storage, signature, index, audit-log, or provenance-enforcement runtime code.
Split proposal mechanics are anchored by supervisor split payload work-item construction and regression tests that mention SG-SPEC-0025.
Continuation selection semantics are anchored by linked-continuation runtime derivation and tests that mention SG-SPEC-0026.
Reflective execution readiness composition is anchored by proposal runtime follow-up derivation and SG-SPEC-0027 regression tests.
Proposal/split mechanics coordination is anchored by split work-item construction that carries split mechanics into proposal handoff readiness.
Proposal/split availability gating is anchored by split target validation and SG-SPEC-0029 regression tests.
Proposal/split prerequisite readiness is anchored by implementation work readiness blockers and SG-SPEC-0030 non-bypass regression tests.
Proposal/split gateway prerequisite consumption is anchored by the implementation work next-gap policy that maps blocked prerequisite readiness to one bounded attach_trace_baseline path.
Proposal/split mechanics readiness region is anchored by branch-rewrite split-pressure routing that exposes emit_split_proposal as one reviewable capability response.

Deterministic continuation candidate selection is anchored by linked-continuation derivation that admits only canonical frontier facts and treats proposal/task/runtime surfaces as non-deriving review context.

Proposal/refactor queue parent-contract semantics are semantic-surface backed by the runtime evidence registry and a live regression anchor. Concrete queue behavior remains delegated to SG-SPEC-0039 and SG-SPEC-0041, while runtime queue functions remain evidence markers rather than a dedicated SG-SPEC-0034 code embodiment.

Proposal queue retry-condition semantics are semantic-surface backed by the runtime evidence registry and a live regression anchor. This trace contract intentionally does not claim queue scheduler, retry executor, canonical writeback, or authority-transition runtime code.

Active-item precedence queue-control semantics are semantic-surface backed by the runtime evidence registry and a live regression anchor. This trace contract intentionally does not claim queue scheduler formulas, queue engine internals, canonical writeback, or authority-transition runtime code.

Non-retry queue-control field semantics are semantic-surface backed by the runtime evidence registry and a live regression anchor. This trace contract intentionally does not claim runtime queue scheduling, governance execution, canonical storage, or dedupe implementation code.

Queue field-surface and artifact-shape contracts are semantic-surface backed by the runtime evidence registry and a live regression anchor. This trace contract intentionally does not claim value semantics, runtime queue execution, scheduling, canonical writeback, or authority-transition code.

Non-retry queue-control cluster semantics are semantic-surface backed by the runtime evidence registry and a live regression anchor. This trace contract intentionally does not claim concrete field semantics, artifact-shape execution, first-persistence implementation, scheduler, or canonical writeback code.

Non-retry queue-control first-persistence and consumption-boundary semantics are semantic-surface backed by the runtime evidence registry and a live regression anchor. This trace contract intentionally does not claim concrete field semantics, artifact-shape execution, runtime queue scheduling, canonical writeback, or authority-transition code.

Retry-condition and active-item precedence gateway semantics are semantic-surface backed by the runtime evidence registry and a live regression anchor. This trace contract intentionally does not claim queue engine behavior, persistence implementation, concrete retry execution, active-item scheduling formulas, or authority-transition code.
Proposal/split gateway boundary is anchored by graph-next-moves routing that treats tracked proposal coverage as the active readiness boundary over branch rewrite residue.
Proposal/split readiness admissibility is anchored by proposal-lane coverage that suppresses branch split residue only when the tracked lane provides the non-bypass path.
Proposal/split gateway entry is anchored by graph-next-moves ranking that preserves explicit split-readiness boundary review actions ahead of tracked proposal-lane availability for the same subject.
Subtree-shape and lower-boundary handoff signals are currently embodied in supervisor graph-health derivation and tests.
Canonical presence-state handling is embodied in supervisor active-vs-historical topology logic and tests.

Proposal/split readiness verdict semantics are anchored by graph-next-moves source facts that gate queued split signals on applicable proposal status, keep stale split-readiness evidence unresolved, and classify active proposal-lane availability as satisfied.
Proposal/split reviewer action semantics are anchored by graph-next-moves source facts that map readiness verdicts to bounded reviewer dispositions without expanding into mechanics detail.

Proposal/split readiness discrimination checkpoints are anchored by graph-next-moves verdict logic that allows not_available only when an explicit reviewable negative availability source is present and keeps missing negative-source evidence unresolved.
